title: THE NAME — DIRECTOR'S CUT V6 FIXED

memory_name_01.title: IDENTITY RECONSTRUCTION
memory_name_01.description: Rebuild the erased seventh crew member from recovered fragments. Wrong answers destabilize the archive.

events.text: Access spine is tighter than the schematic promised.
I know. Shocking. A technical diagram lied to us.
Smells like hot plastic, old dust, and something I am officially not naming until I have to.
Noah said you already saw his name.
If that's true, it's buried in records we've already touched.
And before we start pretending yesterday didn't happen—
if you asked me what I did back there, I remember.
That is not the same thing as being fine with you.
The memory index is three junctions ahead.
I'm stepping over them, not on them.

event.speaker: MAYA
event.text: The wall just clicked. I hate walls with timing.

events.text: There's a maintenance drone in the wall.
I can check it, or keep moving before my courage files a complaint.
Low ceiling. Narrow walls. Deeply rude to knees.
If I get stuck, delete this from any heroic version of the transcript.

event.speaker: MAYA
event.text: Cable brushed my neck. I handled that with dignity. You cannot prove otherwise.

events.text: There are old cable tags in here.
Rivera hated handwriting on system labels.

choices.text: IGNORE IT AND REACH THE INDEX.

events.text: Someone forced it open from the outside.
There's a route packet tucked under the diagnostic core.
That does not belong in a maintenance drone.
Nothing today belongs where it belongs.
I need both hands to pull it without shredding the buffer.
Someone rerouted a person like a circuit.
I hate that it made sense.
I'm peeling the tag loose. Carefully.
The station keeps offering little reasons to stop.
If that turns out stupid, please remember I looked determined.
Somebody wanted this shut even if the station woke up.
Or bad news pretending to be a temperature.
I can force it, or scan the lock first.
That makes it an operational decision, apparently.
Tamper loop under the manual latch.
If I forced this, the local cache would've wiped itself.
Which is exactly the sort of petty behavior I expect from this place.
Like a person who absolutely slept this week.
More like it's trying to get attention without looking desperate.

events.title: CORRUPTED MANIFEST FRAGMENT
events.content:
CREW MEMBER #07
STATUS: ERASED
SURNAME: VOSS
MEMORY INDEX: DAMAGED
SOURCE CONFLICT: OFFICIAL MANIFEST DENIES ENTRY
events.caption: Partially recovered crew record from the personnel memory index.
events.message: [CORRUPTED MANIFEST FRAGMENT ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

events.text: I don't remember anyone named Voss on ECHO.
I don't like how easy that was to say.

event.speaker: UNKNOWN
event.text: YOU REMEMBER THE SHAPE OF THE HOLE.

events.text: But I know what that name can do in this system.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: Names are not labels in ECHO's identity layer.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: Some wake things that were shut down for a reason.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: Maya, take your hand off the core.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: Denied. Sealed. Filed under Omega clearance conflict.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: That is institutional language for: do not ask while anyone important is listening.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: I do not remember the answer.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: That fact is becoming less comforting by the minute.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: Hale can panic in complete sentences.
I am going to make the dead computer talk.
Also, Ethan, if this kills me, I am haunting your punctuation.
That means he either hated the joke or is worried.

events.title: MAINTENANCE LOG
events.content:
Relay inspection completed.
Signal bleed detected on emergency carrier.
Manual reset recommended.

Signed: N. Voss
events.caption: Recovered relay maintenance entry with partial signature.

events.text: Maintenance log gives us N. Voss.
Noah is starting to look less like a ghost story.
More like a person someone removed very carefully.
And if you're wondering whether I am okay with that sentence—

choices.text: TELL MAYA TO STEP BACK FIRST.

events.text: It keeps redirecting the query into an empty crew slot.
No, I don't have a better verb.
I hate that I needed someone else to say that.
Just enough to make small buttons feel personally insulting.
I have to keep the core awake manually.
If I let go, it drops the fragment and we start over.
So I'm standing here with one hand on a hot memory core.

e06_waiting.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Maya is holding the memory core open. No new choices until she returns.

e06_waiting.devNote: Legacy fallback node. Not connected to main story flow because characterBusy resumes through returnNodeId.

events.text: My glove is warm from the core housing.
Close enough to make me polite.
We have the shape of him now.

events.title: IDENTITY FRAGMENT
events.content:
CREW MEMBER #07
SURNAME: VOSS
INITIAL: N.
ROLE CLASS: COMMUNICATIONS
MEMORY INDEX STATE: SUPPRESSED
TRANSFER STATE: CONTRADICTORY
events.caption: Recovered identity fragment assembled from memory index and maintenance records.

events.text: If we enter the name, the system may restore the profile.
Or it may let something answer to it.
Give it exactly what the records support.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: The system will accept confidence it has not earned.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: That is how bad archives become worse gods.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: If you do this, do it cleanly.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: We are not inventing a person.
Role class: communications. The recovered answer is Noah Voss.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: I wish saying that did not feel like turning a handle from the wrong side of a door.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: Okay. I will walk you through it.
Not just Voss. Not just Noah if the panel gets picky.
And if the archive screams, we pretend that is normal and keep our hands steady.

events.title: NOAH VOSS
events.content:
NOAH VOSS
ROLE: COMMUNICATIONS SPECIALIST
CLEARANCE LEVEL: OMEGA
STATUS: UNKNOWN
TRANSFER STATUS: DENIED
MEMORY INDEX: SUPPRESSED
events.caption: Reconstructed personnel profile from fragmented archive records.
events.message: [NOAH VOSS PROFILE ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

events.text: There is a second after the profile opens where nobody says anything.
Because a deleted man just got his name back.
I don't know whether to be relieved or furious.
The first person erased from ECHO was the one whose job was to make sure people could speak.

events.title: TRANSFER RECORD: NOAH VOSS
events.content:
TRANSFER REQUEST: NOAH VOSS
ROLE: COMMUNICATIONS SPECIALIST
STATUS: DENIED
DATE: 21 DAYS BEFORE LAUNCH
REASON: OMEGA CLEARANCE CONFLICT
FINAL NOTE: DO NOT ASSIGN TO ECHO
events.caption: Recovered transfer record showing Noah Voss was officially denied station assignment.
events.message: [NOAH VOSS TRANSFER RECORD ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

events.text: Then why was his name inside our station?
Why was his handwriting in our walls?
Why does the archive flinch when we say him out loud?
Not where they let you remember me.
events.speaker: NOAH

events.text: I was the voice behind the static.
events.speaker: NOAH

events.text: The one Hale never told you about.
events.speaker: NOAH

events.text: Drone tried to wipe the packet when I touched the core.
Nothing panics unless it has something to hide.

events.title: DRONE ROUTE CACHE
events.content:
ROUTE CACHE / PARTIAL
DESTINATION: PERSONNEL MEMORY INDEX
RECENT QUERY FRAGMENT: ...VOSS...
OPERATOR: UNKNOWN
CACHE STATUS: DAMAGED
events.caption: Recovered route packet from damaged maintenance drone.
events.message: [DRONE ROUTE CACHE ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

events.text: Voss was not just in a manifest layer.

events.title: HANDWRITTEN CABLE TAG
events.content:
HANDWRITTEN LABEL RECOVERED
TEXT: VOSS / MEMORY INDEX BYPASS
INK AGE: PRE-BLACKOUT
AUTHORIZED LABEL FORMAT: INVALID
events.caption: A handwritten cable tag found inside the service crawl.
events.message: [HANDWRITTEN CABLE TAG ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

events.text: I hate how personal this is starting to feel.
Just a sealed hatch and the feeling that I skipped something that wanted to be found.
Also sparked directly into my sleeve.
My sleeve has filed a disagreement.
And my shoulder is going to remember your leadership style.
That is now my favorite kind of door.
I am choosing to believe caution is not always cowardice.

upgradeFocus: Episode 05 trust fracture is now visible in Maya's opening tone.
Major decisions now create dialogue-visible consequences, not only stat changes.
Maya remembers accusations, injuries, caution, skipped clues, and route decisions.
Busy durations reduced and returns rewritten as concrete reports.
Noah reveal strengthened into a sharper cliffhanger.
Every visible choice still has exactly two options.
System messages remain minimal and evidence-focused.
Lore is delivered through physical evidence, route discoveries, and character reaction.

episodeObjective: Guide Maya to the personnel memory index, recover Noah Voss identity fragments, make the Episode 05 trust fracture visible in dialogue, and decide whether Noah is preserved as evidence or allowed to keep channel 7.14 open.

summary: Concrete episode goal remains Noah Voss identity reconstruction.
Maya stays physically active and reports environment, sound, smell, temperature, fear, and physical strain.
Player choices produce visible emotional and operational consequences.
Some clues can be missed permanently.
Maya can become colder toward the operator after trust-breaking choices.
Hale, Maya, and Noah have distinct voices.
First event, then character reaction, then player choice.

fixedIssues: Reduced e06_n11_wait characterBusy duration from 600000 ms to 240000 ms.
Renamed the long memory recovery busy id from v6 to v7-style id for clarity.
Marked e06_waiting as explicit legacy fallback so unreachable-node reports do not look like story-flow errors.
